qtype: Ratio division, unit conversion, reverse percentage, fraction of a quantity
tex:
\begin{question*}{13}{a}An orchard has 1250 trees.
\newline
The trees are in the ratio apple : pear : cherry = 12 : 9 : 4.
\newline

\begin{subpartsaliged}
Calculate the number of apple trees.
\newline

Last year in the orchard, the mean mass of fruit produced was 64 kg per tree.
\newline

Calculate the total mass of fruit produced last year.
\newline
Give your answer in tonnes.
\newline
[1 tonne = 1000 kg]

Last year, the mean mass of pears produced was 54 kg per tree.
\newline
This was a decrease of 10\% on the mean mass of pears produced per tree from the year before.
\newline

Calculate the mean mass of pears produced by each pear tree the year before.
\newline

The orchard loses $\frac{1}{5}$ of its total number of trees in a storm.
\newline

Calculate the number of trees that remain.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b)Paulo buys some pears from a market.
\newline
Pears cost \$0.54 each or 0.51 euros each.
\newline

\begin{subpartsaliged}
Paulo pays in dollars for 12 pears.
\newline

Calculate the change he receives from \$10.
\newline

The exchange rate is \$1 = 0.826 euros.
\newline

Calculate how much more Paulo pays for each pear when he pays in euros.
\newline
Give your answer in dollars, correct to the nearest cent.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

\end{question*}

tex:
The diagram shows a solid triangular prism $ABCDEF$ of length 15 cm.
\newline
$AB = 6.4$ cm, $EB = 5.7$ cm and the volume of the prism is 145 cm$^3$.
\newline

(a) Show that angle $EBA = 32^\circ$, correct to the nearest degree.
\newline

(b) Find the length of $EA$.
\newline

(c) Calculate the shortest distance from $E$ to $AB$.
\newline

(d) Calculate the angle $BF$ makes with the base, $ABCD$, of the prism.
\newline

(e) The prism is made of plastic with density 938 kg/m$^3$.
\newline

Calculate the mass of the prism in grams.
\newline
[Density = $\frac{\text{mass}}{\text{volume}}$]

\newline

\newline

tex:
\begin{question*}{13}{a}The table shows information about the mass of each of 1000 eggs.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Mass ($m$ grams) & $40 < m \leqslant 50$ & $50 < m \leqslant 56$ & $56 < m \leqslant 64$ & $64 < m \leqslant 70$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 126 & 520 & 154 & 200 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\begin{subpartsaliged}
Calculate an estimate of the mean.
\newline

An egg is picked at random from the 1000 eggs.
\newline

Find the probability that this egg has a mass greater than 56 g.
\newline
Give your answer as a fraction in its simplest form.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b)One year, a farmer makes a profit of \$$24\,730$ selling eggs.
\newline

Write this profit

\begin{subpartsaliged}
correct to 2 significant figures

in standard form.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(c)On a farm, there are 500 hens, correct to the nearest 10.
\newline

\begin{subpartsaliged}
In one year, the mean number of eggs laid per hen was 320 eggs, correct to the nearest 20.
\newline

Calculate the upper bound for the total number of eggs all the hens lay in that year.
\newline

Another farm has 800 hens, correct to the nearest 20.
\newline

Calculate the lower bound for the difference between the number of hens on the two farms.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

\end{question*}

\end{question*}

tex:
\begin{question*}{13}{a}There are 160 people in a village.
\newline
The cumulative frequency diagram shows information about their ages.
\newline

\begin{subpartsaliged}
Find an estimate for

(a) the median age

(b) the lower quartile

(c) the number of people who are 50 or more years of age

(d) the 65th percentile.
\newline

The youngest person in the village is 1 year old and the oldest is 70 years old.
\newline

(a) Draw a box-and-whisker plot to show the distribution of ages in the village.
\newline

(b) Write down an estimate of the percentage of people in the village that are younger than the median age.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b)The frequency table shows information about the age of each person in another village.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Age ($n$ years) & $0 < n \leqslant 20$ & $20 < n \leqslant 30$ & $30 < n \leqslant 50$ & $50 < n \leqslant 80$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 52 & 37 & 24 & 60 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
On the grid, complete the histogram to show this information.
\newline
The first block has been drawn for you.
\newline

\end{question*}

\newline

\newline

\end{question*}

qtype: Coordinate geometry calculations (midpoint, length, gradient, line equation)
tex:
\begin{question*}{14}{a}In the square $ABCD$, $A$ has coordinates $(-2, 1)$ and $B$ has coordinates $(1, 5)$.
\newline
$C$ has coordinates $(a, b)$, where $a$ and $b$ are both positive integers.
\newline

Find the coordinates of $C$ and the coordinates of $D$.
\newline
You may use the grid to help you.
\newline

(b)$P$ has coordinates $(-1, 3)$ and $Q$ has coordinates $(6, 4)$.
\newline

\begin{subpartsaliged}
Find the coordinates of the midpoint of $PQ$.
\newline

Find the length of $PQ$.
\newline

Find the gradient of $PQ$.
\newline

Find the equation of the line parallel to $PQ$ that crosses the $x$-axis at $x = 2$.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

\end{question*}

qtype: factorisation, algebraic fractions, solving rational and quadratic equations, change subject of formula
tex:
\begin{question*}{18}{a}Factorise fully.
\newline

\begin{subpartsaliged}
$27y^2 - 3$

$2m - pk + 2k - pm$

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b)Solve $\frac{x-1}{x+1} - \frac{6}{x-1} = 1$.
\newline

(c)Solve $4x^2 - 3x - 2 = 0$.
\newline
You must show all your working and give your answers correct to 2 decimal places.
\newline

(d)Make $k$ the subject of the formula.
\newline
\[ \frac{k}{m} = 4 + kp \]

\end{question*}

content.content:
Solve $\frac{x-1}{x+1} - \frac{6}{x-1} = 1$.
\newline

(c)Solve $4x^2 - 3x - 2 = 0$.
\newline
You must show all your working and give your answers correct to 2 decimal places.
\newline

(d)Make $k$ the subject of the formula.
\newline
\[ \frac{k}{m} = 4 + kp \]

\end{question*}

qtype: forming and graphing inequalities, feasible region
tex:
A tailor makes $x$ dresses and $y$ shirts in one week.
\newline
In one week

(a) he makes at least 4 dresses

(b) he makes no more than 7 shirts

(c) he makes less than 14 dresses and shirts altogether

(d) the number of shirts he makes is more than $\frac{2}{3}$ of the number of dresses.
\newline

One of the inequalities that shows this information is $x \geqslant 4$.
\newline

(a) Write down the other three inequalities in $x$ and/or $y$.
\newline

(b) 
On the grid, draw 4 straight lines and shade the unwanted regions to show these inequalities.
\newline
Label the region $R$ that satisfies the 4 inequalities.
\newline

(c) Use your diagram to find the smallest number of dresses and the smallest number of shirts the tailor makes in one week.
\newline

(d) The profit the tailor makes on one dress is \$10 and the profit on one shirt is \$6.
\newline

Use your diagram to find the largest profit the tailor can make in one week.
\newline

content.content: he makes at least 4 dresses
he makes no more than 7 shirts
he makes less than 14 dresses and shirts altogether

the number of shirts he makes is more than $\frac{2}{3}$ of the number of dresses.
\newline

stem.content:
A tailor makes $x$ dresses and $y$ shirts in one week.
\newline
In one week

$C = \{ x : x \text{ is a multiple of } 3 \}$

\newline

qtype: function evaluation, inverse functions, composite functions, solving exponential equations
tex:
\[ f(x) = x - 4   g(x) = 2x + 5   h(x) = 3^x \]

(a) Find
(i) (b) $f(-3)$

(c) $g^{-1}(x)$

(d) $f(g(f(x)))$

(e) Find $x$ when $h(x) = g(f(2))$.
\newline

content.content: Find

Find $x$ when $h(x) = g(f(2))$.
\newline
